searchDocs.title: Cryo
searchDocs.content: Cryo A collection of methods for working with immutable data in a functional way. Operations return new tables rather than mutating their inputs. The library is split into two namespaces: Cryo.Dictionary — operations on string-keyed tables. Cryo.List — operations on array-like tables. Cryo.None is a sentinel that, when passed as a value to dictionary merges, removes the corresponding key from the result.

searchDocs.title: promiseCharacter​
searchDocs.content: </> CharacterPromiseUtils.promiseCharacter(player: Player ) → Promise<Model > Returns a promise that will resolve once a character exists.

searchDocs.title: disconnectAndClear​
searchDocs.content: </> disconnectAndClear.disconnectAndClear( connections: {RBXScriptConnection }-- Array of connections to disconnect and clear. ) → ()

searchDocs.title: keys​
searchDocs.content: </> Dictionary.keys() → () Returns a list of the keys from the given dictionary.

searchDocs.title: map​
searchDocs.content: </> Dictionary.map() → () Returns a new Dictionary with keys and values passed through a mapper function. Returning a second value in the mapper function will reassign the key.

searchDocs.title: values​
searchDocs.content: </> Dictionary.values() → () Returns a list of the values of the given dictionary.

searchDocs.title: omit​
searchDocs.content: </> Dictionary.omit() → () Returns a Dictionary which excludes the given keys.

searchDocs.title: filter​
searchDocs.content: </> Dictionary.filter() → () Returns a Dictionary of only entries for which the predicate function returns true.

searchDocs.title: join​
searchDocs.content: </> Dictionary.join() → () Combine a number of dictionary-like tables into a new table. Keys specified in later tables will overwrite keys in previous tables. Use Cryo.None as a value to remove a key. This is necessary because Lua does not distinguish between a value not being present in a table and a value being nil.

searchDocs.title: bindToInstanceDestroyed​
searchDocs.content: </> bindToInstanceDestroyed.bindToInstanceDestroyed( instance: Instance ,-- The instance to watch. callback: () → ()-- Invoked exactly once after instance is destroyed. ) → () Registers a callback that runs once instance is destroyed.

searchDocs.title: IsLocalDev​
searchDocs.content: </> IsLocalDev.IsLocalDev( devEnvName: string-- Identifier of the local dev environment to look for. ) → boolean Returns true when devEnvName appears anywhere in the current stack traceback. The check is a literal substring match, not a pattern.

searchDocs.title: safePlayerAdded​
searchDocs.content: </> safePlayerAdded.safePlayerAdded( callback: (Player ) → ()-- Invoked for each existing and future player. ) → RBXScriptConnection -- The PlayerAdded connection. Disconnect to stop receiving future callbacks.

searchDocs.title: create​
searchDocs.content: </> ElttobLog.create( devEnvName: string-- Identifier passed to IsLocalDev and used as the log prefix. ) → ( tag: string, enabled: boolean ) → { info: (...any) → (), warn: (...any) → () } Returns a (tag, enabled) -> { info, warn } constructor bound to devEnvName. Loggers built from it only emit output when running inside a matching local dev environment and enabled is true.

searchDocs.title: enable​
searchDocs.content: </> input.enable( v: Context, n: number?-- Increment amount; defaults to 1. ) → () Increments the enable count of a context by n (default 1). The context's underlying InputContext.Enabled becomes true once the count is positive.

searchDocs.title: disable​
searchDocs.content: </> input.disable( v: Context, n: number?-- Decrement amount; defaults to 1. ) → () Decrements the enable count of a context by n (default 1). The underlying InputContext.Enabled becomes false once the count drops to zero or below.

searchDocs.title: context​
searchDocs.content: </> input.context( name: string,-- Name applied to the underlying InputContext. v: Context ) → Context Reconciles a context (and any nested contexts/actions it contains) into actual InputContext instances. Returns the same table you passed in for chaining/storing.

searchDocs.title: action​
searchDocs.content: </> input.action( v: Action-- An action descriptor with a type discriminator. ) → ActionTypes Reconciles an action descriptor into an InputAction instance and returns it augmented with the ActionFunctions API (is_pressed, is_released, is_changed, get_state, plus pressed, released, and state_changed signals). The return type is narrowed by the type field on the descriptor: "bool" → ActionBool, "1d" → Action1D, etc.

searchDocs.title: binding​
searchDocs.content: </> input.binding(v: Binding) → Binding Identity helper that returns its argument typed as a [Binding]. Useful for declaring bindings inline with type inference.

searchDocs.title: clear_state​
searchDocs.content: </> input.clear_state() → () Clears the per-frame transient state (is_pressed, is_released, is_changed flags) for every action. Call this once per frame before polling so each press/release/state-change is observed exactly once.

searchDocs.title: capture_input​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> input.capture_input() → { bind: Enum.KeyCode, modifier1: Enum.KeyCode, modifier2: Enum.KeyCode } Yields until the user produces input, then returns a table containing the primary KeyCode along with up to two modifier keys held during the press. Mouse buttons and touch are translated to their KeyCode equivalents.

searchDocs.title: TypeValidation
searchDocs.content: TypeValidation Runtime type validators for common Roblox value types. Each validator returns true when its input matches the expected shape and false otherwise, making it suitable for assertion-style precondition checks. Exposed validators: validateCFrame validateInstance validateNumber validateSimpleTable validateVector3

searchDocs.title: debug​
searchDocs.content: </> SimpleLogger.debug: (...any) → () Studio-only debug logging — silent in published experiences.

searchDocs.title: info​
searchDocs.content: </> SimpleLogger.info: (...any) → () Informational logging via print.

searchDocs.title: warn​
searchDocs.content: </> SimpleLogger.warn: (...any) → () Warning logging via warn.

searchDocs.title: ButtonSpec​
searchDocs.content: </> type ButtonSpec = { id: string, title: string, tooltip: string?, iconId: string?, clickableWhenViewportHidden: boolean?, onClick: () → () } Describes a button to register on the shared toolbar. id must be unique within the calling peer.

searchDocs.title: RegisterOptions​
searchDocs.content: </> type RegisterOptions = { plugin: Plugin , mediumName: string, toolbarTitle: string, peerId: string, protocolVersion: number?, buttons: {ButtonSpec} } Options passed to SharedToolbar.register. plugin — your plugin instance. mediumName — a name shared by every cooperating plugin; the BindableEvent parented under StudioService uses this as its name. toolbarTitle — the title to advertise. The peer with the lowest peerId wins, so authoritative naming is stable across reshuffles. peerId — a stable identifier for this plugin (e.g. its name). protocolVersion — optional override for the wire protocol version. buttons — list of buttons to contribute.

searchDocs.title: SharedToolbarHandle​
searchDocs.content: </> interface SharedToolbarHandle { setActive: ( self, buttonId: string, isActive: boolean ) → () setEnabled: ( self, buttonId: string, isEnabled: boolean ) → () destroy: (self) → () } Returned by SharedToolbar.register. The methods only affect this peer's own buttons; calling them on another peer's id errors.

searchDocs.title: register​
searchDocs.content: </> SharedToolbar.register(options: RegisterOptions) → SharedToolbarHandle Joins (or creates) a shared toolbar identified by options.mediumName and contributes the buttons in options.buttons to it. Returns a SharedToolbarHandle for managing this peer's button state. Cleans up automatically when options.plugin unloads.

searchDocs.title: toSet​
searchDocs.content: </> List.toSet() → () Create a dictionary where each value in the given list corresponds to a key in the dictionary with a value of true.

searchDocs.title: map​
searchDocs.content: </> List.map() → () Create a copy of a list where each value is transformed by callback.

searchDocs.title: removeValue​
searchDocs.content: </> List.removeValue() → () Creates a new list that has no occurrences of the given value.

searchDocs.title: findWhere​
searchDocs.content: </> List.findWhere() → () Returns the index of the first value for which predicate(value, index) is truthy, or nil if not found.

searchDocs.title: foldRight​
searchDocs.content: </> List.foldRight() → () Performs a right-fold of the list with the given initial value and callback.

searchDocs.title: removeRange​
searchDocs.content: </> List.removeRange() → () Remove the range from the list starting from the index.

searchDocs.title: foldLeft​
searchDocs.content: </> List.foldLeft() → () Performs a left-fold of the list with the given initial value and callback.

searchDocs.title: filter​
searchDocs.content: </> List.filter() → () Create a copy of a list with only values for which callback returns true. Calls the callback with (value, index).

searchDocs.title: removeIndex​
searchDocs.content: </> List.removeIndex() → () Remove the element at the given index.

searchDocs.title: find​
searchDocs.content: </> List.find() → () Returns the index of the first value found or nil if not found.

searchDocs.title: reverse​
searchDocs.content: </> List.reverse() → () Returns a new list with the reversed order of the given list.

searchDocs.title: getRange​
searchDocs.content: </> List.getRange() → () Returns a new list containing only the elements within the given range.

searchDocs.title: join​
searchDocs.content: </> List.join() → () Joins any number of lists together into a new list. Entries equal to Cryo.None are skipped.

searchDocs.title: replaceIndex​
searchDocs.content: </> List.replaceIndex() → () Returns a new list with the new value replaced at the given index.

searchDocs.title: filterMap​
searchDocs.content: </> List.filterMap() → () Create a copy of a list doing a combination filter and map. If callback returns nil for any item, it is considered filtered from the list. Any other value is considered the result of the 'map' operation.

searchDocs.title: sort​
searchDocs.content: </> List.sort() → () Returns a new list, ordered with the given sort callback. If no callback is given, the default table.sort will be used.

searchDocs.title: Categories​
searchDocs.content: The packages are split into two top-level groups:  Modules — runtime libraries for Roblox experiences (immutable data, player utilities, type validation, decompression, and more).Plugin Libraries — helpers built specifically for Roblox Studio plugins.  Browse the API tab in the navigation bar for the full list, or jump straight to a package via its sidebar entry.

searchDocs.title: Installing​
searchDocs.content: Add the packages you need to your wally.toml:  [dependencies] Cryo = "morgann1/cryo@1.1.1" SimpleLogger = "morgann1/simple-logger@0.1.2"   Then run wally install to fetch them into your project.

searchDocs.title: gunzip​
searchDocs.content: </> zzlib.gunzip( str: string-- Gzip-compressed input. ) → string-- Decompressed output. Decompresses a gzip-formatted byte string and returns the raw bytes.

searchDocs.title: inflate​
searchDocs.content: </> zzlib.inflate( str: string-- Zlib-compressed input. ) → string-- Decompressed output. Decompresses a zlib-formatted byte string and returns the raw bytes.

searchDocs.title: files​
searchDocs.content: </> zzlib.files( buf: string-- The full ZIP archive bytes. ) → () Returns a stateless iterator over every entry in a ZIP archive. Each iteration yields (name, offset, size, packed, crc) for one file header. Use zzlib.unzip to extract a specific entry.

searchDocs.title: unzip​
searchDocs.content: </> zzlib.unzip( buf: string,-- The full ZIP archive bytes. arg1: string | number,-- Filename to extract, or raw offset. arg2: number?-- Expected CRC when arg1 is a numeric offset. ) → string-- Decompressed file contents. Extracts a single file from a ZIP archive. When arg1 is a string, it's treated as the filename to extract; the function searches the archive and returns the decompressed bytes, or errors if the file isn't present. When arg1 is a number, it's treated as a raw offset into buf and arg2 is the expected CRC.
